headline: Trump went big on tech stocks in first quarter of 2026, new filings show
summary: President Trump bought shares of Amazon, Meta, Oracle, Broadcom, Motorola and Dell worth millions, new ethics disclosure filings show.

headline: Trump Bought the Software Stock Slump—and More Revelations From His Trades
summary: President Donald Trump has been busy in the stock market.  Trump disclosed purchases and sales of hundreds of millions of dollars worth of U.S. stocks in the first quarter, according to a disclosure released by the U.S. Office of Government Ethics.  Trump purchased between $1 million and $5 million worth of  ServiceNow Adobe Workday  on Feb. 10, the disclosure revealed.

headline: Figma: Adobe Is Just A Better Alternative (Even After Q1)
summary: Figma, Inc. delivered strong Q1 results, with 46% YoY revenue growth and notable customer expansion, but FIG valuation remains demanding. Learn more about FIG stock here.

headline: Figma’s Earnings Just Flipped the Narrative
summary: Figma (NYSE: FIG) has had one of the wildest post-IPO rides in recent memory. After debuting at $33 in July 2025, the stock soared to nearly $143 before crashing to around $20. That’s an 88% collapse from peak levels, despite the business itself continuing to grow rapidly.
